_README: X1 directed reconciliation (plan §1.5). Maps 24 v2 rows onto pre-existing Rocq constants in digraph-theory (written in the pre-monorepo directed campaign), verified faithful by the x1-reconcile-directed workflow (run wf_05f12221: per-file matcher + independent second-reader faithfulness check; only both-agree-faithful rows kept). Merged by build_v2_manifest.py: sets formal_name/repo=digraph-theory/phase=X1/already_formalized + the source-verification tuple, and drives the v2 overlay statement=done. re-encodings are technique-#3 independent encodings of the same row (recorded, not the canonical formal_name). followups_* are X2 work: proxy = an existing NON-faithful constant (row stays todo — do not mark partial for a vacuous/weaker encoding); unmatched = an existing constant with no corpus row (candidate to mint a row or an OPG/settled-theorem to leave alone).

axv_1605_07411_00.note: forall oriented H: chi_bounded_under (oriented G /\ ind_free H G) <-> oriented_forest H. Encodes Conj 2 'Forb(H) is chi-bounded iff H is a forest'. chi_bounded_under = exists f, chi(U(G)) <= f(omega(U(G))) [the paper's undirected chi/omega of the underlying graph, per docstring]; ind_free = no induced-subdigraph copy (arc-preserving-and-reflecting injection); oriented_forest = underlying graph of

axv_1605_07411_01.note: forall oriented star S: chi_bounded_under (oriented G /\ ind_free S G). Encodes Conj 4 'for any oriented star S, Forb(S) is chi-bounded'. oriented_star = oriented + a centre c urel-adjacent to every other vertex with leaves pairwise non-adjacent = an orientation of K_{1,t}. Class = oriented graphs avoiding an induced S. Matches the row exactly. Faithful.

axv_2310_04265_10.note: Rocq: forall k, 3<=k -> forall N, exists T:tournament, kcritical k T /\ N < #|T|. Encodes AACL (arXiv:2310.04265 v2) Conjecture 5.10 'For every integer k>=3, there are an infinite number of k-omegabar-critical tournaments.' kcritical (critical.v) = (omegabar T == k) && forall v, omegabar(del_tournament v)==k-1, exactly the paper's k-omegabar-critical definition. Quantifier nesting and the k>=3 gua

axv_2310_04265_09.note: Rocq: exists ell:nat->nat, forall (T:tournament)(k:nat), k<=omegabar(T) -> exists S:{set T}, #|S|<=ell k /\ k<=omegabar(sub_tournament S). Encodes AACL Question 5.9 (positive form): 'Is there a function ell such that, for every tournament T, if omegabar(T)>=k then T has a subtournament A with |A|<=ell(k) and omegabar(A)>=k.' Single function ell (f=id), object=tournament, sub_tournament S = induced

axv_2310_04265_07.note: Rocq: exists f ell:nat->nat, forall (T:tournament)(k:nat), f k<=domnum T -> exists S:{set T}, #|S|<=ell k /\ k<=omegabar(sub_tournament S). Encodes AACL Conjecture 5.3 (Large dom implies an omegabar-cluster) = manifest #07, whose statement_text ('two functions f and ell ... every tournament T with dom(T)>=f(k) contains a subtournament X with |X|<=ell(k) and omegabar(X)>=k') matches verbatim. domnu

axv_1608_03040_00.note: `forall D:diGraphType, exists col:D->'I_3, majority_col col`, where majority_col = [forall v, 2*#|same_col_outnb col v| <= outdeg v] and same_col_outnb = out-neighbours w with col w = col v. This is exactly Conjecture 2 (Kreutzer-Oum-Seymour-van der Zypen-Wood, 1608.03040): 'Every digraph has a majority 3-colouring'. 2*#same<=outdeg encodes 'at most half of out-neighbours share the colour' faithfu

axv_1608_03040_01.note: `forall k, 2<=k -> forall D, exists col:D->'I_k.+1, kmajority_col k col` with kmajority_col k = [forall v, k*#same <= outdeg v]. Matches Conjecture 9 (1608.03040): for k>=2 every digraph has a (k+1)-colouring with at most (1/k)deg+(v) same-coloured out-neighbours. k+1 colours (I_{k.+1}), k*#same<=outdeg <=> #same<=deg+/k, guard 2<=k all match. (Row status is 'disproved' mathematically; encoding is

axv_1608_03040_03.note: `forall T:tournament, exists col:T->'I_3, majority_col col`. Matches Open Problem 2 (1608.03040): 'Does every tournament have a majority 3-colouring?'. Object is the HB `tournament` structure (finite irreflexive complete digraph, core/tournament.v); majority_col reused. Faithful.

axv_1608_03040_04.note: `forall D, eulerian D -> exists col:D->'I_3, majority_col col` with eulerian = [forall v, indeg v == outdeg v]. Matches Open Problem 3 (1608.03040): 'Does every Eulerian digraph have a majority 3-colouring?'. Caveat: `eulerian` encodes only the balanced (in=out at every vertex) condition, dropping the classical connectivity requirement; but majority colouring is a per-vertex local condition preser

Encodes Conj 3.12 'the class of tournaments with twin-width <= k is chi-vec-bounded': dicolorableb T m = chi-vec(T)<=m, omegabar T = omega-bar(T), tww_le is the concrete contraction-sequence twin-width. Encodes Conj 3.13 'exists f s.t. every T has an ordering with omega(T^p)<=f(omega-bar) AND tww(T,p)<=f(omega-bar)': bclique p = omega(T^p) backedge clique number, both conjuncts present with correct exists-f/forall-T/exists-p structure.
